name: South Indian Fish Curry
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs pomfret (I've done it with pomfret. It's s, fish)
3 tbsp butter (ghee is better, ghee == clarified butter)
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the onions and tomatoes.
2. Make a paste of the turmeric, cumin, ginger, and chili peppers.
3. Clean and cut the fish into manageable pieces.
4. Heat the butter/ghee in a pan. Add the chopped onions and tomatoes, then stir in the spice paste.
5. Cook for 15 minutes until the onions are translucent.
6. Add salt and one can of coconut milk. Stir and cook for five minutes.
7. Add the fish pieces and mix well.
8. Pour in the vinegar and the remaining can of coconut milk.
9. Bring to a boil, then remove from heat. Serve with rice.
10. Garnish with coriander leaves, sliced onions, tomato slices, and lemon wedges if desired.
